theory.concept:
"Gaps" means finding missing values in a sequence (e.g. missing dates); "islands" means finding consecutive runs of related values (e.g. consecutive days someone was active).

- The standard islands technique: assign a row number to the ordered data, then subtract it from the actual value — rows in the same consecutive run end up sharing the same difference, which lets you group them
- This trick (value - ROW_NUMBER() = constant within a run) comes up in several disguises once you recognize the pattern
- Finding gaps is usually simpler: check whether value + 1 exists in the table; if it doesn't, the next value is missing
theory.syntax: SELECT column, column - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY column) AS grp FROM table_name;

shared_table.name: Logins

questions.prompt: For each user, group their login days into consecutive islands using the row_number-minus-value trick.
Using the grouping from Q1, find the start and end day of each consecutive login streak per user.
Find the missing login day for user 1 (a day with no login between their earliest and latest login).
